resources.string: Pick a resolver, then Start. Check the small box on a server to include/exclude it from the Quick Settings tile\'s cycle \u2014 tapping the tile only cycles through checked servers.
\uD83D\uDD11 Root detected \u2014 tap to enable system DNS (no VPN, no ADB)
Primary IP (optional if using DNS-over-TLS below)
Off by default. When on, keeps the last 300 queries in memory only \u2014 nothing is written to disk, and it clears when the app is killed.
Every app uses the device-wide default DNS unless you give it its own. Tap an app to assign it a specific resolver.
All apps use the device-wide default

Pick a provider below. Both reconnect on the interval you set further down \u2014 Mullvad picks a new server/country each time, Cloudflare WARP just reconnects (no server picking, but a fresh connection can land on a different Cloudflare edge).
Mullvad \u2014 real servers, country rotation (needs a one-time anonymous account number)
Cloudflare WARP \u2014 zero signup, reconnects on a timer (no country picker)
Enter your Mullvad account number and tap Register device first \u2014 this is a one-time setup, not a recurring login.

Intervals under ~30s spend a noticeable chunk of each cycle just reconnecting the tunnel, and very frequent reconnects may get rate-limited by the provider\'s servers. 30s\u20135min is more reliable for regular use.

Uses Cloudflare\'s unofficial WARP registration API to generate a completely anonymous WireGuard identity \u2014 nothing typed in, ever. Reconnects to your nearest Cloudflare edge on the interval set below; there\'s no server or country list to pick from, but each fresh connection can land on a different edge IP. Being unofficial, this is less stable long-term than Mullvad \u2014 see README if it stops working.
Not set up yet \u2014 tap above, no information needed.
